content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4

## Summary

Found two correctness gaps in the exhaustion handling: process-backed backends still miss stdout-only exhaustion messages, and OpenRouter still leaves generic 402/payment-required responses retryable unless the body happens to match the new keyword list.

## Amendments

1. `src/adapters/process_backend.rs:1456`, `src/adapters/process_backend.rs:1495`, and `src/adapters/process_backend.rs:1843` still never feed raw stdout into exhaustion classification on non-zero exits; they only pass `extract_stdout_error(...)`, which means stdout-only quota/usage-limit failures that are not Claude `is_error` envelopes still fall back to `TransportFailure` and burn the full retry budget. Add a guarded raw-stdout fallback for classification when no structured stdout error was extracted.
   Rationale: The feature explicitly needs stderr/stdout exhaustion detection. The current callers knowingly skip plain stdout errors, so a backend that reports quota exhaustion on stdout will still retry instead of degrading.
2. `src/adapters/openrouter_backend.rs:218`, `src/adapters/openrouter_backend.rs:223`, and `src/adapters/openrouter_backend.rs:354-381` only mark OpenRouter exhaustion when the response body matches the keyword list. A bare HTTP 402 Payment Required or a generic payment/billing body that misses those exact substrings is still treated as `BackendUnavailable(None)` at probe time or `TransportFailure` at invoke time, so the run retries instead of degrading. Classify status 402 itself, or at least a small generic payment-required/billing set, as `BackendExhausted`.
   Rationale: HTTP 402 is already a terminal payment failure. Requiring a matching body string makes graceful degradation brittle and leaves a common exhaustion response path retryable.
